w:r.w:t: A world-class page builder is not a monolithic application — it is an extensible platform. ClickFunnels, Webflow, WordPress Gutenberg, and Shopify all share a common architecture: a small, fast core + a rich plugin ecosystem.
This PRD defines the plugin system architecture for our page builder. The system enables us to:
Ship a lean, fast core editor while deferring specialized features to plugins
Enable third-party developers to extend the builder (marketplace opportunity)
Iterate quickly on new features without touching core code
Support multiple business models (freemium, premium plugins, white-label)
Match ClickFunnels' extensibility while maintaining Laravel-native simplicity

The editor core handles: canvas rendering, drag-drop, inspector panels, state management, and save/publish. Everything else — analytics integrations, A/B testing, advanced animations, third-party connectors, AI content generation — is a plugin.
18 essential core plugins for Phase 1 launch
1.2 What This PRD Does NOT Cover
Page builder core features (see separate PRD)
Specific business logic for individual plugins (each gets its own spec)
Marketplace business model (pricing, revenue share — separate doc)
Editor loads in <1.5s because it ships without analytics, A/B testing, AI tools, etc. Users only download what they enable.
Team can ship analytics plugin while another team ships AI plugin — no merge conflicts, independent release cycles.
Each plugin has isolated tests. Easier to debug. Failed plugin doesn't crash core.
Update Google Analytics integration without touching editor core. Roll back bad plugin without rollback entire app.
Plugins can be toggled on/off per workspace. Perfect for beta testing or enterprise-only features.
Core free/cheap, premium plugins paid. E.g., 'Advanced Analytics Plugin - $29/mo'.
Marketplace where agencies/developers sell plugins (we take 20-30% cut). WordPress model.
Once ecosystem grows, switching cost for users is high (they lose their plugins). Network effect.
Agencies can disable our branding plugin, enable their own logo plugin. Customization without forking.
Ship MVP fast, iterate via plugins. Don't wait for 'perfect' monolith.
Plugin Registry • Lifecycle • API • Sandboxing • Marketplace
Analytics • A/B Test • AI Writer • Zapier • Stripe • Custom Elements • ...
Plugin files uploaded to /plugins/{plugin-slug}/, manifest parsed, DB record created, dependencies checked
Plugin enabled for workspace, init() hook called, settings panel shown, assets enqueued
Plugin registers hooks, adds elements/panels/buttons, listens to events
Plugin disabled, cleanup() hook called, UI elements removed, event listeners unregistered
Plugin files deleted, DB records removed, user data optionally deleted (with confirmation)
Adds new draggable elements to the editor. Examples: Video Player (Wistia), Advanced Form Fields, Image Carousel, Interactive Quiz, Product Grid (Shopify), Testimonial Rotator, AI Image Generator
Connects builder to third-party services. Examples: Google Analytics, Facebook Pixel, Zapier, Stripe Checkout, Mailchimp, ActiveCampaign, Slack, HubSpot, Calendly, Gong
Extends core editor capabilities. Examples: A/B Testing, Heatmaps, Session Recording, Advanced Animations, Custom Fonts, Global Blocks Library, Version Control, Multi-Language, AI Content Writer
Bundles pre-designed templates/sections. Examples: E-commerce Pack (10 product page templates), Agency Pack (portfolio templates), SaaS Pack (pricing pages), Holiday Promo Pack
At launch (Phase 1), we ship 18 essential plugins bundled with the builder. These are maintained by us, not third-party. They demonstrate the plugin API and provide immediate value.
Inject GA4 tracking script, track page views, events, conversions. Config: GA Measurement ID.
Track page views, custom events (form submit, button click), conversions. Config: Pixel ID.
Fire custom events to any endpoint on user actions. Config: Event name, webhook URL, element selector.
Send form submissions to Zapier webhook. Config: Webhook URL, field mapping.
Auto-add form leads to Mailchimp list. Config: API key, list ID, tag.
Embed Stripe payment button. Config: Publishable key, product ID, success URL.
Add PayPal smart button element. Config: Client ID, currency.
Adds YouTube video element with privacy mode, autoplay, controls config.
Adds Vimeo video element with custom player colors, privacy.
Search and insert free stock photos from Unsplash API directly in editor.
Carousel of testimonials with auto-rotate, star ratings, photos.
Shows 'X people viewing this page' (real or simulated). Config: Real/fake, min/max range.
Pre-designed trust icons: SSL, money-back guarantee, free shipping, secure checkout.
Urgency timer (fixed date, evergreen, daily reset). Config: End date, action on expire.
Show popup when user tries to leave. Config: Trigger, delay, popup content.
Fixed bottom/top bar with CTA button. Config: Text, button, position, scroll trigger.
Add global custom CSS per page. Config: CSS editor with syntax highlighting.
Cookie consent banner with Accept/Reject. Config: Text, styling, consent storage.

/plugins/ google-analytics/ plugin.json ← Manifest plugin.php ← Backend logic (Laravel) plugin.js ← Frontend logic (Alpine/Vue) README.md screenshot.png ← Marketplace thumbnail stripe-checkout/ plugin.json plugin.php plugin.js views/ settings.blade.php assets/ icon.svg
Every plugin has a plugin.json manifest:
Plugins register via Laravel service provider:

<?php
// plugins/google-analytics/plugin.php

namespace Plugins\GoogleAnalytics;

class GoogleAnalyticsPlugin extends BasePlugin
{
    public function init()
    {
        // Called when plugin activates
        $this->registerHook('page.render', [$this, 'injectScript']);
    }

    public function injectScript($page)
    {
        $measurementId = $this->getSetting('measurement_id');
        return "<script async src='https://www.googletagmanager.com/gtag/js?id={$measurementId}'></script>";
    }

    public function cleanup()
    {
        // Called on deactivate
    }
}
Plugins hook into the editor lifecycle via events. Hooks are organized into categories:
element.add, element.remove, element.update, element.select, element.click (published page)
toolbar.register (add button), panel.register (add sidebar), menu.register (add menu item)
Plugins interact with the editor via the Plugin API:
Add new draggable element to library. Config: name, icon, category, defaultContent, inspector fields.
Listen to editor events. Callback receives event data.
Add button to top toolbar. Config: icon, label, onClick handler.
Add custom tab to inspector panel. Config: label, component (Vue/Blade).
Get plugin setting value (from user config).
Display toast notification to user. Type: success, error, info, warning.
Detailed specifications for the 18 core plugins we ship at launch.
Track page views, custom events, conversions with GA4
Injects GA4 script in <head>, auto-tracks pageview, sends events when user clicks elements with data-event attribute
1. Install plugin, 2. Enter Measurement ID, 3. Activate, 4. Done — all published pages now tracked
Add Stripe payment buttons to pages
Publishable Key (text), Secret Key (password, stored encrypted)
Stripe Button — Config: Product ID, Price, Success URL, Cancel URL
Loads Stripe.js, renders checkout button, redirects to Stripe Checkout on click
Webhook endpoint to handle payment success/failure, updates order status
Countdown Timer — Config: End date/time, Mode (fixed/evergreen/daily), End action (hide/redirect/message), Style preset (4 options)
JavaScript timer updates every second, calculates time remaining, executes end action, stores evergreen start in localStorage
Fixed (absolute date), Evergreen (X hours from first visit), Daily (resets at midnight)
Enable per page (toggle), Delay (seconds), Show once per session (toggle)
Adds 'Exit Popup' panel in inspector where user builds popup content using mini-builder
Detects mouse leaving viewport (desktop) or back button (mobile), shows modal overlay with content, tracks if shown in sessionStorage
Headline, text, form (email capture), button, close X
Send form submissions to Zapier webhooks
Adds 'Zapier Webhook' option in form submit actions. User pastes Zapier webhook URL.
On form submit, POSTs JSON to webhook URL with field data, shows success/error message
Auto-maps form fields to JSON keys (email → email, name → name, custom_field_1 → custom_field_1)
Testimonial Rotator — Config: Add testimonials (name, photo, quote, rating), Auto-rotate (toggle + interval), Layout (carousel / grid / single)
Renders testimonials, auto-rotates if enabled, prev/next arrows, swipe support on mobile
Testimonials (repeater: name, photo upload, quote textarea, rating 1-5), Auto-rotate (toggle), Interval (slider 3-10s), Layout (3 presets)
(Remaining 12 core plugins follow similar spec structure — full specs in separate technical docs)
These plugins ship 3-6 months post-launch (Phase 2). More complex, higher value, potential upsell.
Create page variants, split traffic, track winner. UI: Variant manager, traffic allocator, stats dashboard.
Visual heatmap of clicks, scrolls, mouse movement. Integration with Hotjar/Crazy Egg or custom tracker.
Record user sessions (mouse, clicks, scrolls). Privacy-compliant (mask sensitive fields). Playback UI.
Generate headlines, copy, CTAs with GPT-4. UI: Right-click element → 'AI Generate'. Config: OpenAI API key, tone, length.
Scroll-triggered animations (fade, slide, zoom, parallax). UI: Animation panel in inspector, timeline editor (simple).
Build pages in multiple languages. UI: Language switcher, translation manager, auto-translate via DeepL API.
Save sections as global blocks; edit once, update everywhere. UI: Block manager, Insert from library, Sync status.
Git-style versioning. UI: Commit with message, view history, diff viewer, restore version, branch/merge (advanced).
Remove our branding, add agency logo/colors. UI: Branding panel (logo upload, primary color, hide 'Powered by').
Sync form leads to HubSpot contacts. UI: OAuth connect, field mapping, trigger selection.
Display Shopify products in grid. UI: Connect Shopify, select collection, customize card layout.
Embed Calendly widget. UI: Enter Calendly URL, customize appearance (inline/popup).
To encourage third-party plugins, we provide:
Plugin Development Guide (50-page documentation with examples)
Sandbox environment (test plugins without affecting production)
Plugin review process (security audit + QA before marketplace approval)
Revenue share: 70% developer, 30% us (if sold via marketplace)
PSR-12 (PHP), ESLint (JS), no eval(), proper escaping, error handling
No XSS, CSRF, SQL injection. API keys encrypted at rest. User data consent.
JS bundle < 100KB (gzipped). No blocking scripts. Lazy-load where possible.
README with setup, config, screenshots. Changelog. Support contact.
Minimum 70% code coverage. E2E tests for critical flows.
GPL-compatible or proprietary (if paid). Clear terms.
1. Developer submits plugin via marketplace portal (uploads .zip)
2. Automated checks: manifest valid, dependencies met, code scan (static analysis)
3. Manual review: security audit, UX check, conflict test (doesn't break core or other plugins)
4. QA testing: install, activate, test on 3 sample pages, deactivate, uninstall
6. If approved: plugin goes live in marketplace; developer notified
7. Ongoing: monitor reviews, support tickets; flag for re-review if issues arise
Users discover and install plugins via the marketplace (built into editor):
Layout: Grid of plugin cards (icon, name, rating, price, install button)
Filters: Category (Element, Integration, Enhancement, Template), Price (Free, Paid), Rating (4+ stars), Official (our plugins) vs. Third-party
Search: Full-text search across name, description, tags
Plugin detail page: Screenshots, description, changelog, reviews, support link, install button
Install flow: Click Install → confirm permissions → download → activate → configure (settings panel opens)
YouTube Embed, Basic Analytics, Custom CSS Injector — no cost, unlimited use
Countdown Timer: Free with basic features, Pro version ($9/mo) unlocks evergreen mode + advanced styling
Advanced Animations Pack: $49 one-time, lifetime updates
A/B Testing Plugin: $29/mo, includes stats dashboard + unlimited variants
AI Content Writer: Free tier (10 generations/mo), $19/mo for 500 generations
For paid plugins sold via our marketplace:
We take 30% (covers payment processing, hosting, support, review overhead)
Payouts monthly via Stripe Connect (min. $50 balance)
Developer dashboard shows sales, revenue, downloads, reviews
Plugins are third-party code running in our app. Potential threats:
Malicious plugin steals user data (API keys, page content, analytics)
Plugin injects XSS into published pages
Plugin makes unauthorized API calls (spam, DDOS)
Plugin conflicts break editor or other plugins
Plugins declare required permissions in manifest (analytics, tracking, storage, http). User sees permissions on install and approves.
Plugin API calls rate-limited: 100 req/min (HTTP), 1000 storage ops/min. Prevents abuse.
Plugin localStorage/DB isolated per plugin. Plugin A cannot read Plugin B's data.
Plugins cannot use eval(), new Function(), or dynamic script injection. Enforced via static analysis.
Content Security Policy blocks inline scripts, restricts script sources to plugin-approved domains.
All marketplace plugins manually reviewed for security before approval (see section 9.3).
If plugin is flagged malicious post-launch, we can remotely deactivate it for all users (emergency only).
Plugins that collect user data must:
Declare data collection in manifest and marketplace listing
Obtain explicit user consent (checkbox on activation)
Encrypt sensitive data at rest (API keys, tokens)
Not sell or share user data with third parties without consent
Each plugin has a settings panel where users configure it:
Layout: Modal or sidebar (depending on complexity)
Fields: Auto-generated from manifest settings schema (see section 5.2)
Save button: Saves settings, validates, shows success/error toast
Simple text input. Example: API Key, Measurement ID
Masked input, stored encrypted. Example: Secret Key
Multi-line text. Example: Custom CSS, Tracking Script
Number input with min/max. Example: Interval (3-10)
Add/remove rows of fields. Example: Testimonials (name, photo, quote)
Plugins need to store configuration and state. We provide three storage mechanisms:
User configuration (API keys, preferences). Stored in DB, encrypted if sensitive.
Plugin runtime data (cache, temp state). Accessed via storage.get/set API. Stored in Redis.
Plugin data attached to specific page (e.g., GA tracking code for this page). Stored in page JSON.
Plugins can slow down the editor if not managed well. Performance rules:
Lazy-load plugins: Only load when activated (not all installed plugins at once)
Code-split: Plugin JS/CSS loaded on-demand (not in main bundle)
Async initialization: Plugins init in parallel, not blocking editor load
Priority queue: Core plugins load first, third-party plugins load after editor.ready
MAJOR: Breaking changes (e.g., API signature changes, removed features)
1. Developer uploads new version to marketplace
2. System checks: version > current, manifest valid, tests pass
3. If MAJOR version: users notified 'Update available, may break compatibility. Review changelog.'
4. If MINOR/PATCH: auto-update (or user can toggle 'auto-update' off)
5. Changelog shown in plugin detail page
6. Rollback option: user can revert to previous version if update breaks something
We provide comprehensive docs for plugin developers:
Quick start guide, plugin structure, hello-world example, boilerplate CLI
Full manifest schema, field descriptions, validation rules
All API methods (registerElement, registerHook, etc.), parameters, return values, code examples
Complete hook catalog, event payloads, timing diagrams, when to use each
How to add toolbar buttons, inspector tabs, panels, modals. Vue/Blade component examples.
Input sanitization, XSS prevention, API key encryption, permissions model
How to write unit/E2E tests, sandbox environment, debugging tools
Submission process, requirements checklist, review timeline, common rejection reasons
10+ real plugin examples (Element, Integration, Enhancement) with full source code on GitHub
Plugin system architecture doc, API design, manifest schema, security model, DB schema for plugin registry
Plugin registry backend, lifecycle (install/activate/deactivate/uninstall), hook system, API surface, sandboxing/permissions, settings UI generator
Build 18 core plugins (see section 7), test integration, documentation, settings panels
Marketplace UI (browse, search, install), developer portal (upload, manage), review workflow, payment integration (Stripe Connect)
Developer documentation site, API reference, examples, video tutorials, boilerplate CLI
Invite 50 beta developers, collect feedback, iterate on API, approve first 10 third-party plugins
Public marketplace launch, marketing (blog, docs, email), monitor for issues
Build 12 advanced plugins (A/B testing, AI writer, heatmaps, etc. — see section 8)
Total timeline: ~35 weeks (8-9 months) to full plugin ecosystem
Minimal plugin that injects GA tracking script on published pages.
Adds a draggable countdown timer element.
